generated_oml:
// Sensing Components
instance InternalHeatbedTemperatureSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Temperature sensor located near the heatbed inside the styrofoam enclosure, providing real-time temperature readings."
    DTDFVocab:producedData InternalHeatbedTemperatureDataTransmitted
]

instance InternalFanTemperatureSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Temperature sensor located near the fan inside the styrofoam enclosure, providing real-time temperature readings."
    DTDFVocab:producedData InternalFanTemperatureDataTransmitted
]

instance ExternalAmbientTemperatureSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Temperature sensor located outside the styrofoam enclosure, monitoring ambient temperature."
    DTDFVocab:producedData ExternalAmbientTemperatureDataTransmitted
]

// Data Transmitted
instance InternalHeatbedTemperatureDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom InternalHeatbedTemperatureSensor
]

instance InternalFanTemperatureDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom InternalFanTemperatureSensor
]

instance ExternalAmbientTemperatureDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom ExternalAmbientTemperatureSensor
]

// Data
instance InternalHeatbedTemperatureData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Time-series temperature data point from the internal heatbed sensor, stored in the time-series database."
    DTDFVocab:fromData InternalHeatbedTemperatureDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo RabbitMQ
]

instance InternalFanTemperatureData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Time-series temperature data point from the internal fan sensor, stored in the time-series database."
    DTDFVocab:fromData InternalFanTemperatureDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo RabbitMQ
]

instance ExternalAmbientTemperatureData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Time-series temperature data point from the external ambient sensor, stored in the time-series database."
    DTDFVocab:fromData ExternalAmbientTemperatureDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo RabbitMQ
]

// Models
instance ModelA : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Differential-equation based simulation model with two free parameters, used for initial calibration and validation."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo CalibrationProcess
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SimulationEngine
]

instance ModelB : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Differential-equation based simulation model with four free parameters, capturing heatbed inertia and preferred for further DT development."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo CalibrationProcess
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SimulationEngine
]

// Enablers
instance RabbitMQ : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Message broker facilitating communication between the low-level driver, controller, and digital twin services."
    DTDFVocab:enables RealTimeMonitoringService
    DTDFVocab:enables CalibrationService
    DTDFVocab:enables AnomalyDetectionService
    DTDFVocab:enables WhatIfSimulationService
    DTDFVocab:enables SelfAdaptationService
]

instance CalibrationProcess : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "On-demand calibration routine that adjusts free parameters of ModelB using a tracking simulator."
    DTDFVocab:enables CalibrationService
]

instance SimulationEngine : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Co-simulation framework that runs what-if simulations using ModelA and ModelB with historical or live data."
    DTDFVocab:enables WhatIfSimulationService
]

instance KalmanFilter : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "State estimator that uses ModelB to estimate unmeasured heatbed temperature and detect anomalies."
    DTDFVocab:enables AnomalyDetectionService
]

instance TimeSeriesDatabase : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "InfluxDB instance storing telemetry data for real-time dashboards and model training."
]

instance Dashboard : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Grafana-based visualization platform displaying temperature, actuator states, and controller status."
]

instance BangBangController : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Bang-bang controller implemented on the Raspberry Pi, controlling heatbed and fan with parameters LL, UL, H, C."
    DTDFVocab:enables SelfAdaptationService
]

instance LowLevelDriver : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Driver on the Raspberry Pi that reads sensors, validates data, and publishes to RabbitMQ."
]

// Services
instance RealTimeMonitoringService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service that ingests sensor data from RabbitMQ, stores it in the time-series database, and updates the dashboard."
    DTDFVocab:provides RealTimeVisualizationInsight
]

instance CalibrationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service that triggers the CalibrationProcess to adjust ModelB parameters based on recent data."
    DTDFVocab:provides RecalibrateModelBAction
    DTDFVocab:provides ConfidenceMeasuresInsight
]

instance AnomalyDetectionService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service that runs the KalmanFilter to estimate heatbed temperature and flag anomalies."
    DTDFVocab:provides AnomalyDetectionInsight
    DTDFVocab:provides HeatBedStateEstimationInsight
]

instance WhatIfSimulationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service that performs co-simulations to evaluate controller parameter variations and predict temperature trajectories."
    DTDFVocab:provides WhatIfSimulationInsight
    DTDFVocab:provides ReoptimizeControllerParametersAction
]

instance SelfAdaptationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service that orchestrates self-adaptation: schedules experiments, reconfigures controller, re-calibrates ModelB, re-optimizes controller parameters, and updates the controller."
    DTDFVocab:provides SelfAdaptationLoopInsight
    DTDFVocab:provides ReconfigureControllerAction
    DTDFVocab:provides RecalibrateModelBAction
    DTDFVocab:provides ReoptimizeControllerParametersAction
    DTDFVocab:provides UpdateControllerAction
]

// Insights
instance RealTimeVisualizationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Insight providing live plots of temperature, heater and fan status, and controller state."
]

instance AnomalyDetectionInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Insight indicating detected anomalies based on Kalman filter residuals."
]

instance HeatBedStateEstimationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Insight giving probabilistic estimate of heatbed temperature with covariance."
]

instance WhatIfSimulationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Insight showing predicted temperature trajectories under varied controller settings."
]

instance SelfAdaptationLoopInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Insight summarizing the self-adaptation actions taken and their outcomes."
]

instance ConfidenceMeasuresInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Insight reporting Kalman filter covariance and RMSE of calibrated models."
]

// Actions
instance ReconfigureControllerAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Automatic action that updates controller parameters LL, UL, H, C based on self-adaptation decisions."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance RecalibrateModelBAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Automatic action that recalibrates ModelB parameters using the latest data."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance ReoptimizeControllerParametersAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Automatic action that optimizes controller parameters via what-if simulation."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance UpdateControllerAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Automatic action that deploys the updated controller configuration to the Raspberry Pi."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

// Physical Components
instance HeatbedActuator : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Electric heater acting as the primary heat source inside the incubator."
]

instance FanActuator : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Fan providing air circulation to distribute heat within the incubator."
]

instance RaspberryPi : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "Single-board computer running the low-level driver, controller, and digital twin components."
]

instance StyrofoamEnclosure : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "Insulated styrofoam box housing the heatbed, fan, and internal sensors."
]

instance LowLevelDriverComponent : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "Component that reads sensors and publishes data to RabbitMQ."
]

instance BangBangControllerComponent : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "Component implementing the bang-bang control logic."
]

instance KalmanFilterComponent : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "Component performing state estimation and anomaly detection."
]

instance CalibrationProcessComponent : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "Component executing the calibration routine."
]

instance SimulationEngineComponent : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "Component running co-simulations for what-if analysis."
]

instance TimeSeriesDatabaseComponent : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "Component storing telemetry data."
]

instance DashboardComponent : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "Component providing real-time visualization."
]

// Environment
instance IncubatorEnvironment : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:contains HeatbedActuator, FanActuator, RaspberryPi, StyrofoamEnclosure, InternalHeatbedTemperatureSensor, InternalFanTemperatureSensor, ExternalAmbientTemperatureSensor, LowLevelDriverComponent, BangBangControllerComponent, KalmanFilterComponent, CalibrationProcessComponent, SimulationEngineComponent, TimeSeriesDatabaseComponent, DashboardComponent
]


instance virtual_to_physical_interaction : DTDFVocab:VirtualToPhysical [
    base:desc "Control messages from the Digital Twin are sent via RabbitMQ to the Raspberry Pi controller, which implements a bang‑bang control strategy. The controller issues commands to the fan and heatbed through the low‑level driver, following a safe protocol. The low‑level driver executes the actuator commands and publishes the resulting PT state back to RabbitMQ for confirmation and feedback."
]

instance dt_constellation : DTDFVocab:Constellation [
    base:desc "The DT system is orchestrated using an event‑driven architecture centered around RabbitMQ (AMQP) as the message broker. The physical twin (incubator) is instrumented with a low‑level driver that periodically reads temperature sensors and publishes data to RabbitMQ. The controller (running on a Raspberry Pi) consumes temperature data, executes a bang‑bang control strategy, and publishes actuator commands back to RabbitMQ. Digital‑twin components subscribe to the same topics: a calibration service that runs model‑parameter estimation (Model A/B) whenever new data arrives; a monitoring service that checks safety limits and triggers alerts; a Kalman‑filter‑based anomaly detector that estimates the unmeasured heat‑bed temperature; a what‑if simulation engine that performs co‑simulations using the latest model parameters; and a self‑adaptation loop that schedules experiments, re‑calibrates, and re‑configures the controller. All data is stored in a time‑series database (e.g., InfluxDB) and visualised on a dashboard. Resource management is handled by the RabbitMQ broker and the underlying OS; the DT components are loosely coupled, enabling horizontal scaling and fault isolation."
]

instance life_cycle_stages : DTDFVocab:EvolutionStage [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin lifecycle in the incubator case study follows a sequence of phases: (1) Physical Twin (PT) definition – hardware (styrofoam box, heatbed, fan, sensors, Raspberry Pi) and software (controller, low‑level driver, RabbitMQ communication server) are specified and deployed; (2) Model development – two thermal models (Model A with two free parameters, Model B with four free parameters) are derived from first‑principles and simplified assumptions; (3) Calibration – the models are calibrated against experimental data using an automated calibration routine (Section 3.3) that adjusts the free parameters to minimize prediction error; (4) Simulation & validation – calibrated models are run in co‑simulation with the PT, generating predictions that are compared to measured temperatures; (5) Deployment – the calibrated models are embedded in the DT framework, where they are accessed by a Kalman‑filter‑based state estimator, an anomaly‑detector, and a what‑if simulation engine; (6) Monitoring & optimization – real‑time data from the PT are ingested via RabbitMQ into a time‑series database, visualised on a dashboard, and used by the Kalman filter to estimate heat‑bed temperature and detect deviations; (7) Self‑adaptation – when an anomaly is detected, the DT triggers an experiment, re‑configures the controller, re‑calibrates Model B, re‑runs what‑if simulations to optimise controller parameters, and finally re‑deploys the updated controller; (8) Governance – all artifacts (hardware schematics, source code, model definitions, calibration scripts) are version‑controlled in a public GitHub repository (https://github.com/INTO‑CPS‑Association/example‑incubator) and documented in the open‑source project, ensuring traceability and reproducibility."
]

instance fidelity_and_validity_considerations : DTDFVocab:FidelityConsideration [
    base:desc "Model fidelity: Model B, with four free parameters, captures the heatbed’s thermal inertia and provides higher fidelity predictions than Model A.
Validation and verification: the calibration algorithm (section 3.3) adjusts the model parameters to fit measured temperature data; the resulting model is validated by comparing simulation outputs to real‑time sensor data.
Uncertainty: a Kalman filter is employed to estimate the unmeasured heatbed temperature and to detect anomalies; the filter’s covariance matrix quantifies estimation uncertainty.
Error detection and correction: anomalies detected by the Kalman filter trigger the self‑adaptation loop, which schedules a new experiment, re‑calibrates Model B, and updates the Kalman filter parameters.
Quality assurance: the entire digital‑twin framework is open‑source, with automated calibration scripts and documented experiments; the repository includes test data and scripts that reproduce the calibration and simulation results, ensuring traceability and reproducibility."
]

instance dt_technical_connection : DTDFVocab:TechnicalConnection [
    base:desc "The DT and PT communicate over a local network using RabbitMQ as the message broker. RabbitMQ is deployed on premises (or in the cloud) and provides reliable, persistent message delivery. The network topology is a star where the Raspberry Pi and DT components connect to the RabbitMQ broker. No explicit bandwidth or latency requirements are specified, but the system relies on low‑latency message passing to support real‑time control."
]

instance dt_hosting_deployment : DTDFVocab:Deployment [
    base:desc "The Physical Twin runs on a Raspberry Pi that hosts the low‑level driver and controller. RabbitMQ is deployed on the same local network (either on the Pi or a separate on‑premise machine). Digital Twin components (simulation, monitoring, calibration) run on a separate host that connects to RabbitMQ. The deployment is on‑premise, edge‑centric, with no explicit scalability or redundancy mechanisms described."
]

instance horizontal_integration : DTDFVocab:HoriIntegration [
    base:desc "The system integrates with external components via RabbitMQ using the AMQP protocol for intra‑system messaging. Sensor data and actuator commands are exchanged through the low‑level driver and controller over this broker. The DT also writes processed data to a time‑series database for visualization, but no other external enterprise systems or interoperability standards are explicitly referenced in the documents."
]
